title: centaurea sphaerocephala subsp. malacitana
intro: Centaurea sphaerocephala subsp. malacitana is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as ballhead thistle or Malacitan ballhead thistle, characterized by its distinctive spherical flower heads. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and saponins. The health benefits of Centaurea sphaerocephala subsp. malacitana include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a promising natural remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of Centaurea sphaerocephala subsp. malacitana can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional folk medicine to treat a range of ailments.
